notes: v1.5: large-scale automated sourcing pass built a verified job-posting corpus far beyond the v1.0 >=15-posting baseline -- 744 companies verified by real HTTP requests to public Greenhouse/Lever/Ashby/Workday job-board APIs (not scraped, not estimated), yielding 67,956 raw postings classified into the 18 archetypes by a keyword/regex rubric (taxonomy/title-classification-rubric.json) with company-context overrides for documented naming collisions (e.g. 'Solutions Engineer' 3-way collision, verified-consultancy override for Solutions Architect Consulting-Side). Classification was iteratively precision-checked by hand-sampling every archetype's matches, catching and fixing 9 real rubric bugs (both false positives, e.g. bare 'Enterprise Architect' matching internal-IT roles, bare 'CTO' matching 'Executive Assistant to CTO'; and false negatives/recall gaps, e.g. reversed word order 'Software Engineer, Embedded Systems', bare 'Developer Relations') before trusting the final counts. Final per-archetype counts: 13 of 18 archetypes exceed 200 classified postings (Product/Full-Stack SWE 6,208 across 542 companies down to Consulting Engineer/PS 202 across 53 companies); 5 archetypes fell short of the 200-posting stretch goal despite three dedicated gap-fill rounds targeting them specifically (Embedded/IoT 148, Solutions Architect Consulting-side 103, Developer Relations 87, Technical Product Manager 63, Solutions Architect Vendor-side 57) -- reported honestly as apparent genuine scarcity of these title patterns on public ATS boards, not padded to hit the target. All 18 archetypes' research briefs updated with a "Posting corpus scale" section citing exact counts and representative companies; full corpus (company registry, raw postings, classified postings, rubric, precision-fix changelog) committed at docs/research/job-postings-corpus/ for independent reproducibility -- see COUNTS.md. No scoring targets, weights, or confidence flags changed by this pass; it is a sourcing-depth/evidentiary-scale upgrade layered on top of v1.4's confidence fix, not a re-scoring. v1.4: raised Product/Full-Stack Software Engineer's stakeholder_client_comfort (0.5->0.7) and people_management_orientation (0.4->0.7) weights, after a real user reported strong client-facing/people-management answers not meaningfully suppressing a Product/Full-Stack SWE #1 match. Root cause: this archetype has no single weight>=0.8 defining trait (unlike Security/DevRel/ML/Mobile/Data/Platform, which each have a real gate), so it wins largely by exact-match 'free fit' on several dimensions where its own target is neutral (3) -- a structural advantage of generalist archetypes over specialist ones that a 2-dimension weight bump only partially offsets (verified: raw score for the reporting user's profile moved 72.5%->71.1%, still #1, ~1 point ahead of Solutions Architect Consulting-Side and Engineering Management). Flagged as a real, only-partially-resolved limitation, not claimed as fully fixed -- see the scoring-mechanism discussion in conversation with the user. All 10 Phase 2 synthetic personas + all regression tests still pass unchanged. v1.2: (1) split teaching_enjoyment into itself (private teaching) + new public_visibility_comfort (public/social visibility), making public_visibility_comfort Developer Relations' true weight-1.0 defining trait and correctly keeping Customer Success Engineer's teaching_enjoyment high while its public_visibility_comfort stays low. (2) Added 4 new domain-fluency skill dimensions (ml_engineering_fluency, mobile_platform_fluency, data_infrastructure_fluency, cloud_infrastructure_fluency) as real gates for ML Engineer, Mobile Engineer, Data Engineer, and Platform Engineer respectively, mirroring the existing physical_constraint_engineering/adversarial_threat_modeling pattern. Both changes made after a real user's second assessment run showed ML Engineer winning on pure generic temperament (with zero self-reported ML/data background) and Developer Relations scoring high despite answers describing only private, not public, teaching preference. v1.1: rescored account_relationship_ownership as two independent dimensions (account_portfolio_breadth, relationship_continuity) per docs/research/dimensions-v1.1-relationship-split.md, grounded in a 149-posting real-JD research pass triggered by a real user bug report. v1.0 base: merged from 5 parallel cluster-scoring drafts (taxonomy/archetypes-draft-*.json), each scoring its archetypes against all 16 v1.0 dimensions in taxonomy/dimensions.json (target 1-5, weight 0.0-1.0, rationale citing the source brief). confidence is "medium" for Mobile Engineer and Embedded/IoT Engineer per the Phase 1 handoff decision (under-sourced, 9 postings vs. the 15 target); "high" for the other 16. Every archetype's scores object contains all 22 dimension ids in taxonomy/dimensions.json v1.2.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Brief describes taking a product requirement from design/PM handoff through delivery and dealing with ambiguous or changing requirements, but scope typically crystallizes via PM/design partnership rather than being self-defined from a vague business outcome.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Cross-functional collaboration is constant, but most of the week is self-directed feature work between planned syncs.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: On-call is 'common at Series B+ startups and scale-ups, less universal at very early-stage startups' — a fair-rotation expectation, not a defining identity.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Debugging is one of several named activities, with no evidence of routine cross-layer diagnosis under live time pressure.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Senior+ engineers architect end-to-end features and staff+ set technical direction, but center of gravity is feature delivery for one product, not platform-scale systems.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Frontend-lean, backend-lean, and generalist variants coexist under one archetype; generalists 'move across the stack fluidly' at seed/Series A — anchor-3 breadth.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Success centers on 'shipping working software that survives contact with production,' real accountability but not a named business KPI/SLO.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Constant cross-functional collaboration with Product/Design/Platform, but counterparts are internal technical/product peers, not external clients or executives. (weight raised 0.5->0.7 in a targeted fix: high client-facing comfort is a real signal toward SA/FDE/consulting-type archetypes and should be able to meaningfully suppress a pure-generalist-IC match, not just nudge it.)

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Code review and mentorship are named senior activities but private peer-to-peer, not designed public-facing teaching content.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Not a meaningful axis; work organizes around product/team ownership, not named accounts.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Not a meaningful axis; work organizes around product/team ownership, not named accounts.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp section explicitly states 'no variable/commission component' — base+equity by company stage.

coding_intensity.rationale: Shipped code is 'the actual production deliverable' and the defining skill axis distinguishing this archetype from advisory/management roles.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: No travel or client-embed expectation appears anywhere in the brief.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed beyond generic engineering diligence; not a named success criterion.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No hardware/physical-resource constraints appear anywhere in this brief.

people_management_orientation.rationale: IC and management are framed as parallel tracks; most product engineers stay IC-focused. (weight raised 0.4->0.7 in a targeted fix: this is one of the clearest available signals toward the EM track and should carry real weight against a pure-IC match, not be diluted by several neutral moderate-target dimensions elsewhere.)

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: No public content or speaking expectation appears anywhere in the brief.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not a required background for the generalist archetype; ML-specific hands-on experience isn't discussed in the brief.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: A generalist full-stack engineer may occasionally touch mobile, but it's not core to the archetype as scoped.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not core to the archetype; data-pipeline work belongs to the Data Engineer archetype specifically.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Some infra exposure is common for full-stack engineers but building/operating infra itself belongs to the Platform Engineer archetype.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Requires defining a 'minimum viable platform' by gathering requirements from internal customers and iterating, rather than working from a top-down mandate.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Constant cross-team collaboration is real, but this is requirements-gathering/advocacy work, not an inbound page/ticket queue.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Platform Engineer and SRE frequently blend at smaller companies implying some on-call exposure, but developer experience is the defining mandate, not reliability.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Managing cloud infra/IaC/Kubernetes implies real debugging, but success criteria emphasize adoption over live incident diagnosis.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Defining skill: building internal developer platforms and shared infra meant to hold up for many consuming teams at organizational scale — the line distinguishing this from classic DevOps at senior/staff level.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Requires fluency across IaC, Kubernetes, networking, and client-library development — 2-3 adjacent infra layers.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Success is measured by adoption and developer cognitive-load reduction — real internal-customer accountability, but not a production SLO the way SRE is judged.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: 'Platform as a product' — gathering requirements from internal teams and advocacy is defining, not incidental; misconceptions section warns engineers who dislike stakeholder work are a poor fit.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Some internal enablement implied (golden paths, docs) but not framed as public-facing teaching content.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: No named external account structure; internal 'customers' are other engineering teams generally, not counted as a portfolio.

relationship_continuity.rationale: No named external account structure; internal 'customers' are other engineering teams generally, not a relationship with duration.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp section states 'no material sales-style variable comp,' base+equity mirroring general SWE comp.

coding_intensity.rationale: Writes client libraries and infra tooling other engineers consume directly — real hands-on production code, tempered by stakeholder-layer time.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: No travel or physical-embed expectation anywhere in the brief.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not a named focus, distinct from a security-engineering archetype; some baseline access-control awareness implied.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No physical/hardware resource constraints discussed; cost/scale optimization is a cloud-economics concern.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Platform Engineer to EM(Platform/Infra) is a common move given stakeholder-management muscle, but the archetype itself is IC-centered.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Internal-facing role; no public content or speaking expectation described.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope (developer platforms/cloud infra, not ML systems).

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Some adjacency (both build internal infrastructure other teams depend on) but data-pipeline specifics belong to Data Engineer.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Added as a real domain-fluency gate for this archetype, mirroring physical_constraint_engineering/adversarial_threat_modeling's pattern — building and operating cloud infrastructure/internal developer platforms is the literal, hands-on core of the job, not just a related architectural-thinking skill (see systems_design_scale for that separate axis).

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Design/influence/review of architecture and standards involves real scoping judgment, but success is anchored to a defined SLO/error-budget metric, not an open-ended business outcome.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: 2025 SRE Report shows a rising 30% operational-time share; daytime work is regularly interrupted by alerts/incidents even outside formal on-call windows.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Defining trait: 24/7 follow-the-sun on-call rotations, live-outage escalation ownership; misconceptions section states the role 'rewards people who specifically enjoy incident composure.'

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Owning escalation during live outages and driving MTTD/MTTR down under real time pressure is core to the role.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Senior SREs influence architecture and standards broadly, designing resilience patterns across services owned by other teams — strong but reliability-focused rather than platform-building.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Requires coding (Python/Go), distributed systems, and observability tooling fluency across supported services — 2-3 layer breadth.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Central success metric is service availability against SLOs/error-budget adherence — a named, measured production outcome even when upstream code is owned by other teams.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Works jointly with product/dev teams on architecture, but counterparts are internal engineering teams during structured reviews and incidents.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Postmortems/architecture reviews involve internal knowledge-sharing but no public-facing teaching output.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: No account/client structure; SREs own services/systems, not a portfolio of external relationships.

relationship_continuity.rationale: No account/client structure; SREs own services/systems, not named external relationships with duration.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: On-call is 'generally not separately compensated as variable pay' at most companies; bands track standard SWE ladders.

coding_intensity.rationale: 'An engineering role, not a ticket-queue role,' requiring years of Python/automation coding — kept below 5 since roughly half the role is operational/incident work.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Reliability work is about accidental/systemic failure modes, not adversarial threat modeling, which is a separate security-engineering concern.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No hardware/physical-resource constraint discussion in the brief.

people_management_orientation.rationale: A notable minority of SREs move into people management earlier than typical given incident-coordination cross-team muscle, but this is a common exit path, not core to the IC archetype.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: No public content or speaking expectation described.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not core; SRE reliability work spans many service types, not specifically data infrastructure.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: SREs work hands-on with cloud infrastructure and observability tooling constantly, though the archetype's defining trait remains oncall_incident_appetite/outcome_accountability rather than infra-building itself.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Translates data-science/analyst needs into pipeline/schema requirements and makes build-vs-buy calls at senior level, but work is generally scoped around a defined pipeline problem.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Practitioner accounts describe checking a monitoring dashboard before Slack each morning since 'pipeline failures often surface overnight' — daytime work frequently fragmented by reactive firefighting.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Formal on-call/pager rotations documented (Uber: dedicated on-call for 20,000+ pipelines); 87%-blame finding centers on firefighting and being blamed when data breaks.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: 'Diary of a Data Engineer' account shows a nominal one-day task spanning schema discovery, Spark/JVM debugging, and cluster-contention troubleshooting over three weeks.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Senior/staff engineers own platform-wide architecture (warehouse migration, streaming platform) meant to hold up as the company scales.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Requires fluency across ingestion, warehouse/lakehouse design, orchestration, and Spark/Flink — 2-3 adjacent layers.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Accountable for pipeline reliability/freshness SLAs and described as the default blame target when data looks wrong even when root cause is upstream.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Constant collaboration with data scientists, analysts, and PMs to translate needs into pipeline requirements — structured internal stakeholder work.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: No public-facing teaching expectation; internal requirements-translation is the closest analog but stays private.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Not relevant for most postings; a minority consulting-flavored variant involves a small caseload of client partnerships.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Not relevant for most postings; a minority consulting-flavored variant involves closer, longer client partnership.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: 'Overwhelmingly salaried, not commission... a build/operate role, not a revenue-attributed one.'

coding_intensity.rationale: Building/maintaining production ETL/ELT pipelines is the core shipped deliverable, tempered by meeting/triage time.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: No travel/embed expectation for the core archetype.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Data-access governance work mentioned but this is compliance/access-control, not adversarial threat-modeling as a primary lens.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No hardware/physical-resource constraints discussed; cost/storage optimization is cloud-economics, not physical budget.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Career ladder includes a management branch, but this is a standard parallel-track option, not defining.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: No public content or speaking expectation described.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Real adjacency — data engineers frequently build the pipelines that feed ML systems — but the archetype's own scope is explicitly distinguished from ML Engineer at the feature-pipeline boundary, so this is a meaningful but secondary signal, not the defining one.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Added as a real domain-fluency gate for this archetype, mirroring physical_constraint_engineering/adversarial_threat_modeling's pattern — building and operating production data pipelines/warehouse infrastructure is the literal, hands-on core of the job.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Real adjacency (modern data infra is usually cloud-native) but distinct from the archetype's core data-pipeline focus.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Seniority inferred from 'the ability to translate vague business problems into measurable ML objectives' — defining the problem and its success metric is itself a senior-level skill.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: ML Platform/MLOps variant sees 40-70% ops work, but the generalist MLE center-of-gravity is closer to standard SWE planned-interruption cadence.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Production ML requires continuous monitoring/retraining and the MLOps flavor carries real on-call burden, but described as fair-rotation, not a defining identity.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Most time goes to 'building resilient systems that can survive contact with reality' and diagnosing why training data doesn't represent real-world distribution — genuine cross-layer diagnosis, not usually under a live incident clock.

systems_design_scale.rationale: ML Platform/Infra variant requires designing feature stores and serving infra for many ML teams (Uber's Michelangelo: ~400 projects/5,000+ models) — a variant within the archetype, not universal.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Core work spans modeling, data pipelines, APIs, and monitoring ('a full stack developer of the data science').

outcome_accountability.rationale: Success is 'A/B test outcomes and business KPIs moved, not offline model metrics' — a model 'isn't successful because it achieves high AUC, but because it survives the real world.'

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Cross-functional collaboration with product/data science named; ML Platform variant treats other ML engineers as customers.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: GitLab's Staff MLE definition includes acting as external spokesperson, a narrow senior exception, not a primary output for the archetype broadly.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: No account/client structure described; internal 'customers' in the platform variant are other ML/data teams generally, not a portfolio.

relationship_continuity.rationale: No account/client structure described; internal 'customers' in the platform variant are other ML/data teams generally, not a relationship with duration.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp mirrors general software engineering — a title/focus-tag on the generalist SWE ladder, equity not sales-style variable comp.

coding_intensity.rationale: 'The majority of your code is not tied to machine learning' but is still production code (pipelines, APIs, monitoring) that ships.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: No travel/embed expectation discussed anywhere in the brief.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Anthropic's Safeguards MLE role does red-teaming/adversarial robustness work, but this is a specific sub-flavor, not the generalist archetype.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No hardware/physical-resource constraints discussed; compute/GPU budgets are cost-optimization, not hard real-time/power/RF constraints.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Career ladder includes a separate EM(ML) branch, a standard optional parallel track, not defining for the IC archetype.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: GitLab's Staff MLE external-spokesperson duty is a narrow senior exception, not core to the archetype.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Added as a real domain-fluency gate after testing showed this archetype could rank #1 purely on generic senior-engineer temperament (ambiguity tolerance, systems design, coding intensity, outcome accountability) with no check against actual ML/data background — none of those dimensions are ML-specific. Mirrors physical_constraint_engineering (Embedded) and adversarial_threat_modeling (Security), both of which already function as real domain gates.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Real adjacency — most ML engineering work is data pipelines and production hardening, per the brief's own emphasis — but distinguished from Data Engineer by the modeling/serving-layer focus captured in ml_engineering_fluency specifically.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: The ML Platform/Infra variant converges with Platform Engineer's profile, but this is a sub-variant, not the modal case for the archetype.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Cross-functional feasibility/UX tradeoff work with Product/Design, but most work starts from design specs translated into shipped screens.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Code review, mentoring, and sustained feature-building with cross-functional collaboration, none framed as inbound-queue driven.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Crash-reporting triage, staged rollouts, and rollback/kill-switch ownership imply real incident responsibility, but nothing rises to safety-critical/high-stakes-breach level.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Performance work (startup time, memory, battery, jank) crosses app-code and OS/device layers, matching the anchor-3 'crosses a layer or two' description.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Staff+ engineers own architecture across a growing app (modularization, build-time reduction), scoped to one app/team rather than platforms serving many teams.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: iOS/Android kept as one archetype specifically because skillset stays within one platform layer; Staff+ work spans one app's modularization, not multiple platforms.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Success criteria include crash-free rate and App Store rating trend, a real production metric scoped to the shipped app.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Cross-functional work with Product/Design is structured collaboration on feasibility, not client-facing advocacy or executive communication.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Only internal code review and mentoring at senior+ as an incidental duty; no public teaching component named.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: No mention of named client or account ownership; work organizes around app/feature ownership, not a portfolio of relationships.

relationship_continuity.rationale: No mention of named client or account ownership; work organizes around app/feature ownership, not relationship duration.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp explicitly states mobile 'does not carry a distinct comp structure' (base+equity+bonus like other SWE roles).

coding_intensity.rationale: Day-to-day is dominated by building UI/business logic and shipping the actual production app at every level described.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: No client-site travel described; entirely home-base/office engineering.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not a named lens; App Store review/platform-gatekeeping is compliance/process, not adversarial threat modeling.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: Performance budgets (cold-start time, frame drops, app size, battery drain) are 'explicit, measurable, and often gated in CI' — soft resource budgets, not hard pass/fail hardware-boundary work.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Mentoring/pattern-setting named at senior+, but evaluation centers on shipped features/architecture impact, not team growth/retention.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: No public content or speaking expectation described.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Added as a real domain-fluency gate for this archetype, mirroring physical_constraint_engineering/adversarial_threat_modeling's pattern — hands-on native/cross-platform mobile development experience is the literal core of the job, addressing the same finding that motivated ml_engineering_fluency for ML Engineer.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Root-causing hardware-vs-firmware failures is routine and Principal-level scope includes architecting firmware across a product line, but most work starts from a defined product/board requirement.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Occasional international teleconferences outside normal hours at senior+, but the bulk of the role is sustained bring-up/debugging/validation work.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Success criteria frame field diagnostic ability under pressure as core ('why their $2M production line just crashed at 3 AM'), though not necessarily a formal page rotation like SRE/security.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Diagnostic speed across fundamentally different failure domains (hardware, firmware, protocol, timing) is the actual hiring bar — 'a problem-solving discipline, not knowledge recall.'

systems_design_scale.rationale: Principal-level scope is architecting firmware across a product line — real design responsibility but scoped to one hardware product line, not a platform serving many teams.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: EE/CompE backgrounds commonly preferred over pure CS; the field spans bare-metal MCU, embedded Linux, RF/wireless, and safety-critical domains requiring a second formal discipline.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Bugs 'surface as device recalls, warranty claims, or safety incidents' — a direct real-world production outcome the engineer is on the hook for even though hardware defects are outside their control.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Cross-functional leadership between hardware, systems, and QA teams named at senior+, but this is internal cross-team collaboration, not sustained external client-facing work.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: No teaching, content, or public-visibility component appears anywhere in the brief.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: No named client/account relationship structure; work organized around products/boards, not a portfolio.

relationship_continuity.rationale: No named client/account relationship structure; work organized around products/boards, not relationship duration.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Embedded roles disproportionately at non-VC-backed industrial/medical/automotive/defense companies where comp is close to 100% cash — stable-cash-leaning, not commission/utilization-linked.

coding_intensity.rationale: Writing/debugging firmware in C/C++ is the dominant language requirement across nearly every posting reviewed and is the actual shipped deliverable.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Occasional travel to manufacturing partners/international teleconferences at senior+ — a travel dimension largely absent from typical SaaS engineering roles, but periodic rather than majority-embedded.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Testing/validation focus is on hardware failure and compliance, not exploit-oriented thinking, aside from security-hardened firmware as one sub-specialization.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: Power/memory/real-time constraints are 'hard constraints, not nice-to-haves'; 'your elegant software design is worthless' if it violates them, with routine hardware bring-up debugging via oscilloscopes/logic analyzers/JTAG.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Cross-functional leadership/mentoring at senior+/Principal, but success criteria remain centered on field-failure rate, not team growth/retention.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: No public content or speaking expectation described.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope; this archetype's real domain-fluency gate is physical_constraint_engineering, already scored.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Anthropic's Staff+ posting lists 'ability to operate with high autonomy and ambiguity' as a minimum requirement, and threat modeling for AI systems 'whose risks fall outside existing frameworks' is an explicit job task.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Incident response and vulnerability triage arrive on others' timelines, but most day-to-day work (architecture, risk assessments, SDLC consulting) is plannable engineering work.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Detection & Response sub-specialization centers on owning incident response; Anthropic's Staff+ posting names 'standard on-call for incident response' as a job requirement.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Risk/vulnerability assessment and incident postmortems require crossing layers (app code, infra, adversary behavior) under real-time hypothesis-forming.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Designs comprehensive cybersecurity architecture across an organization's systems, but this is architecture-for-defense rather than scaling a system under growing load.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Title splits into cloud/AppSec/network/detection-response sub-specializations, with engineers typically deep in one.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Success ties to 'reduction in exploitable vulnerabilities reaching production' and incident response quality; AppSec engineers increasingly judged on whether developers actually use the guardrails they build.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: AppSec is embedded partnership: 'working closely with engineering teams,' consulting with developers/PMs on security standards, educating teams — explicitly not the 'security cop' stereotype.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Educating teams on secure coding is core, but this is private/1:few enablement of internal teams, not public one-to-many content.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: No named external client/account relationship; work organized around internal engineering-team partnerships, not a portfolio.

relationship_continuity.rationale: No named external client/account relationship; work organized around an anonymous portfolio of vulnerabilities/incidents, not relationship duration.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Pay is 'base-heavy with equity, tracking general software engineering comp discipline' — no sales/utilization-linked variable component.

coding_intensity.rationale: Postings value 'production-grade coding ability' as a direct hiring bar for AppSec, though the role also spans policy/architecture/consulting work.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: No travel or physical site-embed requirement appears anywhere in the brief.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Threat modeling is named as an explicit, primary work product at the frontier (Anthropic's Staff+ posting includes 'threat modeling for AI systems' as core scope).

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No power/memory/timing/RF hardware constraints anywhere; the domain is software/cloud/network.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Staff level mentors engineers and Principal has an ambassadorial role, but ladder criteria center on technical/strategic influence, not team retention/growth.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Educating teams on secure coding is private/1:few internal enablement, not public content.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to the generalist archetype, though a named trust/safety sub-variant does this work — flagged as a minority case, not the modal one.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Cloud security work touches infrastructure, but this archetype's real domain-fluency gate is adversarial_threat_modeling, already scored at weight 1.0.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Runs discovery to scope POC success criteria and improvises live technical objections, but each deal cycle is a defined engagement with a technical-win endpoint, not an open-ended mandate.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Work is organized around scheduled discovery calls, demos, and POCs, with deal urgency creating plannable-but-real interruptions.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Never mentions paging or production incident ownership; risk is reputational/deal-based, not operational on-call.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Debugs live failures in front of the customer with the deal on the line, but this is demo/POC-environment debugging, not production root-causing under an SLA clock.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Designs POC architectures tailored to a prospect's environment, proving fit for one customer's stack, not platforms for many teams.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Must position feature-for-feature against competitors and demo against varied prospect stacks — breadth within one vendor's product applied across many customer domains.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Variable comp tied to team/territory quota attainment, a real external metric, but accountability ends at the pre-sale 'technical win,' not sustained post-sale ownership.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: The entire role is built around discovery calls, tailored demos, and being the technical half of a two-person deal team — central to the archetype's identity.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Explains technical concepts during demos to one prospect at a time; no public content or one-to-many teaching output described.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: The most consistent cluster in a 149-posting review — 100% of substantive SE postings (Saviynt, CoreView, Vantage, Abnormal AI) describe territory/segment coverage paired with an Account Executive, not a single client. CoreView: 'support North American SLED accounts'; Vantage: 'help prospective customers across all segments.'

relationship_continuity.rationale: Predominantly deal/sales-cycle-bounded — each opportunity is transactional — though 'trusted advisor' language (Vantage, Saviynt) shows some SEs maintain relationships across expansion cycles with the same enterprise account, keeping this above the pure anchor-1 floor.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: The defining feature of the archetype: 80/20 or 70/30 base-to-variable OTE tied to quota attainment, with explicit quarter-to-quarter income volatility.

coding_intensity.rationale: Writes custom demo configurations and stands up POC/trial environments, proving fit rather than shipping the production deliverable.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Travel 'varies widely by deal size — enterprise SEs may be on planes weekly... SMB/mid-market SEs may be almost entirely remote.'

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed anywhere; competitive positioning is about product capability, not adversarial/security thinking.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware or physical constraints anywhere in the brief.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Senior/Principal SEs mentor juniors, but success criteria are individual/team-quota-based, not people-outcome based.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: SE's live-demo work is real-time and client-facing but not public one-to-many broadcast — the audience is always a specific prospect, not the public.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Runs discovery workshops and value-stream assessments to define scope, owning multi-year transformation narratives, but work is framed around defined POC/POV deliverables and TCO analyses.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Scheduled consultations and workshops across support/service/sales teams as 'the technical glue' — plannable but frequent cross-team demands.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: No paging or incident-response responsibility described; risk is deal/relationship-based.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Hand-builds POC environments against real customer data/infra under time pressure, a real but bounded-scope diagnostic activity.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Designs future-state architecture and migration plans for large/strategic accounts, but scoped to one customer's environment rather than serving many consuming teams.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Deep on one vendor's stack (e.g., AWS-certified), 'expert in how to apply a particular platform,' applied across many customer domains.

outcome_accountability.rationale: GitLab's handbook ties SA success to sales KPIs (bookings, adoption, win rate), but comp split is 'usually less aggressive than a pure SE's' and engagement is pre-sale-to-early-implementation bounded.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Explicitly the 'trusted advisor to prospects and clients,' running consultations with stakeholders at all levels from engineers to executives.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Produces reusable technical reference content and runs discovery workshops for one account, but no public conference/content component mentioned.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Temporal's Sr Manager, Solutions Architecture posting describes a team 'supporting Scale and Enterprise accounts' (plural) with 'account strategies' (plural) — a territory/segment of many accounts, not one.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Temporal explicitly describes ensuring 'seamless post-sale transitions' to Customer Success/Professional Services — the SA's own relationship is pre-sale/deal-bounded and formally handed off once the deal closes.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Carries variable/incentive comp tied to territory attainment per GitLab's handbook, but 'usually less aggressive than a pure SE's (more base-weighted).'

coding_intensity.rationale: Hand-builds POC/migration environments, but this proves technical fit and produces collateral, not the production deliverable the customer runs on.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: 'Travel to onsite customer meetings and hands-on migration work' at large cloud vendors — regular but not majority-of-weeks embed.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed; competitive positioning and TCO/migration analysis are the focus.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware/power/timing constraints anywhere in the brief.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Ladder branches into SA management at senior levels, but day-to-day success criteria remain individual/account-based.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Client consultations and workshops are private, addressed to a specific account, not public content.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope, though an AI-solutions-specific variant would score higher — not the modal case.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Vendor SAs often work with cloud platforms, but the archetype's defining traits are stakeholder comfort and territory breadth, not hands-on infra-building.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Establishes project boundaries/timeline/budget before a SOW is signed and translates open-ended client priorities into a value proposition, but converges on a defined SOW/blueprint deliverable.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Juggles a portfolio of concurrent billable engagements, workshops, and steering committees rather than an inbound ticket queue.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: No paging or incident-response duties described; risk is engagement/utilization-based.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Architecture review and environment provisioning described, but not live multi-layer production debugging under an SLA clock — design/oversight-oriented per the boundary note vs. Consulting Engineer.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Produces solution blueprints, sequence diagrams, and integration models across multiple environments/cloud stacks — genuine cross-system design, though per-client rather than for many internal teams.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Needs 'breadth across platforms, integration technologies, cloud services, enterprise patterns,' directly contrasted against vendor SAs who go deep on one platform — one of the archetype's most central traits.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Measured by utilization rate and client satisfaction, not a single owned production KPI; 'owns the design that gets handed off to someone else to run.'

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Leads technical discussions, solutioning workshops, and architectural reviews, acting as 'an alignment point between the bid team and the client.'

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Enablement is implicit in workshops with one client's team; no public content or conference speaking documented.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Reinforces the utilization-model finding: Cortex's Sr PS Engineer posting (a close analog) describes a regional 'book of business,' and the archetype's own utilization-rate staffing model (75-85% target) structurally requires juggling a portfolio of concurrent billable engagements — one of the most confidently wide-breadth archetypes in the taxonomy.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Proton.ai's SA posting describes 'clean hand-off to Implementation' after the sales cycle — time-boxed to the SOW/deal, not an indefinite relationship, consistent with the brief's utilization/billable-engagement framing.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Per the resolved comp note, Levels.fyi-anchored figures show a modest bonus (~6-15% of base) tied to utilization/margin, explicitly NOT commission.

coding_intensity.rationale: Thoughtworks postings mandate SAs continue to code alongside developers, but 'codes mainly to prototype and prove technical fit' — coding 'not the bulk of the job.'

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Firm-dependent: 'Accenture and most other Big Four firms carry 50%+ travel' vs. Slalom's local-team model with comparatively less travel.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Security/access-control governance mentioned as one responsibility among many, but not a default reasoning lens.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware/power/timing constraints anywhere in the brief.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Management-track ladder exists (Manager through Partner), but partner-track promotion depends on book of business, not team-outcome metrics.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Client workshops and steering committees are private, account-specific, not public content.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Breadth across cloud platforms is part of the archetype's defining technical_breadth_depth, but hands-on infra-building isn't the deliverable — design and advisory are.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Embeds with 'one customer, many capabilities,' scoping a project's future with a client one week and pair-programming the next, though still within standard engineering rigor.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Week-to-week work varies between conventional SWE work and live client demos/scoping — embed-driven variability rather than an inbound queue.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Participates in production monitoring as a core function, but no formal paging/on-call rotations described.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Real production engineering (PySpark transforms, integration pipelines) with standard code review/monitoring, but not described as time-pressured multi-layer diagnosis the way a pure infra/SRE role is.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Architects technical solutions and integration pipelines for a customer's specific problems — real design work, scoped to one customer's deployment.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: 'Cultivate breadth across systems and industries,' trading depth-in-one-codebase for breadth, ranging across whatever a given customer engagement demands.

outcome_accountability.rationale: The sharpest, most directly sourced anchor-5 case in the cluster: FDE 'owns the customer outcome in production' (vs. SA who owns the design that gets handed off).

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Live-demos to analysts and pair-programs with a client's junior engineer, embedding directly with the customer team, though counterparts skew technical/operational rather than fully executive.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Pair-programming so the client can maintain the system after the engagement ends is real but private/1:1, not public one-to-many teaching.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: FDE breadth is genuinely bimodal by company type, not a single value. Defense/frontier-AI employers (Anthropic: 'embeds directly with our most strategic customers' per engagement; Palantir's canonical 'Delta' model; Defense Unicorns: 'embed with strategic customers' singular) run true single-client embeds. Scaled product companies running FDE as a volume post-sales motion (Cresta, Ramp: 'serve our largest customers' plural, Warp: 'multiple concurrent customer engagements' explicit requirement, OpenRouter: 'a set of post-sales customers') run explicit multi-account portfolios. Target leans toward the archetype's originating Palantir-model case, but weight is deliberately lowered from a would-be 0.9 to 0.5 to reflect this real, evidenced uncertainty rather than false precision — a future pass should revisit whether this archetype needs to split the way Solutions Architect already did into vendor/consulting variants.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Persistence stays confidently high regardless of breadth — even the portfolio-style FDEs describe ongoing relationships (Ramp: 'expand value over time'; OpenRouter: 30/60/90-day onboarding transitioning to 'ongoing account ownership'; Cresta: 'trusted technical advisor for the customer'). This is the dimension where FDE and CSE genuinely agree despite disagreeing sharply on breadth.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: 'The one archetype in this cluster where no quota/commission is the norm, not the exception' — comp structured like core SWE comp.

coding_intensity.rationale: Coding is 'the single biggest time block' of the week, with FDEs spending '70 to 90%' of the week writing production code the client actually runs on.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: '25-50% onsite, up to 25% travel expected at Palantir' with embeds sustained over the life of a deployment.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed; FDE reasoning is framed around deployment/integration engineering.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware/power/timing constraints; FDE work is software integration/deployment.

people_management_orientation.rationale: People-management of an FDE pod exists at senior levels, but core IC success criteria are engineering/deployment-outcome based.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Client-embedded work (demos to analysts, pair-programming) is private to the customer, not public content.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Real adjacency — many current FDE postings are explicitly 'Forward Deployed AI Engineer' roles — but not the modal case across the whole archetype.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: FDEs build integration pipelines as part of customer deployments, a real but secondary adjacency to Data Engineer's core focus.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: FDEs often work with cloud/container infra as part of deployments, but this is in service of the customer outcome, not the archetype's defining trait.

archetypes.name: Customer Support Engineer
archetypes.overall_notes: Reactive, ticket/SLA-driven, queue-based post-sales support archetype. Distinguished sharply from CSE/DevRel by low account continuity, high interrupt/on-call load, and crisp numeric success metrics (SLA/CSAT/FCR).

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Success anchored to explicit numeric SLA/CSAT/FCR targets rather than contested qualitative goals; tickets arrive as discrete, scoped problems.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Work arrives as an inbound ticket/case queue by design — the defining trait of this archetype vs. CSE/DevRel/Consulting.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: PagerDuty's tier covers scheduled SLA/on-call windows including occasional weekends; the Cloudflare CRE variant owns high-severity incidents with weekend on-call.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Reading distributed traces, writing SQL, using feature flags, and shipping a fix within the first response — fast, cross-layer diagnosis under SLA pressure is the core differentiator of the modern version of this job.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Debugging and tooling/script-building described, but not designing platforms for many consuming teams; the CRE boundary case is flagged as an outlier.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Often specializes within a product area but must range across app code, infra, and third-party behavior to debug — moderate, 2-3-layer breadth.

outcome_accountability.rationale: SLA/CSAT/FCR are named, tracked metrics, but accountability is bounded to the ticket/queue level rather than a named account's sustained health.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Structured, ticket-based customer communication and cross-functional partnership, but counterparts are typically technical practitioners case-by-case.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Documentation/runbook improvement is a byproduct of resolving tickets, not a primary evaluated output; no public-facing component.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Explicitly an anonymous, high-volume ticket/case queue model — a large number of different customers move through the queue continuously, the opposite of a stable portfolio of named accounts.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Explicitly an anonymous, interchangeable ticket/case queue model with no named, persistent account ownership — the clearest low-end anchor case in the cluster.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Variable comp is 'typically a modest bonus rather than commission-driven,' structurally decoupled from sales-style outcomes.

coding_intensity.rationale: Real hands-on technical work (SQL, cURL, feature flags, internal tooling), but 'diagnostic/troubleshooting expertise rather than full software-development expertise.'

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Nothing indicates travel or physical embedding; queue/ticket-based and remote-eligible.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Debugging examples (webhooks, SQL, feature flags) are accidental-failure-mode diagnosis, not adversarial/security threat modeling.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware/physical resource constraints; a pure software/SaaS support context.

people_management_orientation.rationale: The IC ladder is the dominant path; management is only one named lateral exit path.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Ticket-based support is private, 1:1 with a specific customer, not public content.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

archetypes.name: Customer Success / Customer Solutions Engineer
archetypes.overall_notes: Proactive, named-account-embedded technical advisor archetype — the deliberate opposite pole from Customer Support Engineer on cadence and account ownership. Source brief is comparatively thin on citation density vs. its Support Engineer sibling, per docs/research/roles-summary.md; split rationale itself is solid.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Success criteria are explicitly qualitative and contested — 'trusted-advisor/relationship quality' is 'less quantifiable than Support's SLA/CSAT metrics,' named by the brief itself as taxonomy-relevant.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Explicitly contrasted against Support's inbound-queue/on-call model: 'scheduled cadence, not shift-based' (recurring check-ins, QBRs, project milestones).

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: On-call described as 'rare here vs. standard there' (Support pole); no on-call rotation or paging structure described.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Resolves complex issues and runs technical workshops, but Databricks' framing has CSEs as 'quarterbacks' coordinating specialist SAs for the deepest technical work.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Not discussed; role is implementation/onboarding project management and enablement design per-account, not platform design.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Needs hands-on product fluency sufficient to run workshops — deep on one vendor's stack applied across many accounts.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Measured on time-to-value and adoption depth for owned accounts, 'rarely comped directly on renewal/expansion' (that's the CSM's number).

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Defined as 'a designated technical contact and trusted advisor' requiring sustained, proactive relationship management over time — a daily mode.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Designing and running workshops and building custom adoption/enablement plans is core, but stays private/1:1 or 1:few with a specific account's team.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Every substantive CSE/CSM/TAM posting reviewed (dbt Labs, LaunchDarkly, Upbound) describes a portfolio/book of many concurrent accounts, never a single deep relationship. LaunchDarkly: 'own a number [of accounts] and forecast weekly'; Upbound: 'map every account,' 'across your accounts.' The archetype's own brief already said 'named book of accounts' (plural), which this dimension's scoring now correctly reflects.

relationship_continuity.rationale: LaunchDarkly's renewal-cycle ownership and Upbound's 'genuine, trusted relationships... own the post-sale relationship' both describe indefinite, ongoing accountability per account — consistent with the archetype's defining framing ('CSEs own a named book of accounts through onboarding, adoption, and ongoing technical health').

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp is 'base-heavy... modest annual bonus, not sales-style commission,' contrasted with quota-carrying CSM/Sales roles.

coding_intensity.rationale: Requires product fluency for workshops, but Databricks routes deep technical specialist work to Solution Architects — advisory/enablement-centric.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Onboarding/QBR cadence implies some periodic in-person engagement without sustained physical embedding.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed anywhere; technical work described is product implementation and enablement.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware or physical resource constraints; a SaaS product-adoption context throughout.

people_management_orientation.rationale: IC-track career ladder; management appears only as a senior-level branch option.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Explicitly the low pole distinguishing this archetype from Developer Relations — CSE's workshops and enablement plans stay private to a specific account's team; the brief's own defining sentence is about a *named account* relationship, never a public audience.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

archetypes.name: Consulting Engineer / Professional Services Engineer
archetypes.overall_notes: Billable, project/SOW-scoped, client-embedded delivery archetype spanning vendor PS orgs and systems integrators. Distinguished by utilization-based variable comp, time-boxed account ownership, and moderate-to-high travel depending on firm model.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Work is scoped by an SOW with a defined deliverable, but engineers navigate scope creep and architecture decisions within that scope.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Organized around structured, plannable cadences (workshops, milestones) rather than an inbound queue, but client escalations create real scheduled interruptions.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Not a named feature; success criteria (utilization, SPI/CPI, margin) are project-delivery metrics, not incident/paging metrics.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Debugging integrations and custom scripting are part of implementation delivery, within a scoped engagement with research time, not a live SLA/audience clock.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Guides architecture decisions for enterprise customer implementations, but scoped to one client's environment/SOW.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Vendor PS roles blend hands-on engineering with support within one product; systems integrators require multi-vendor stack fluency — both cluster around 2-3 adjacent layers.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Held accountable for SOW margin and schedule/cost performance (SPI/CPI) and utilization — real but bounded to a time-boxed engagement's delivery.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Day-to-day centers on technical workshops with client stakeholders as a sustained, daily mode, though counterparts stay technical-to-mid-level more often than fully executive.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Documentation/implementation-standards authorship is private client-specific delivery documentation, not public teaching content.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Every substantive PS/Implementation posting reviewed (Cortex, Yoodli, Warp, Submittable) explicitly requires managing multiple simultaneous client engagements — Warp and Submittable both use the literal phrase 'manage multiple concurrent/simultaneous engagements' as a stated requirement.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Every posting reviewed describes a defined start and end per engagement (Cortex: 'kickoff → delivery → handover'; Submittable: 'kick-off to launch'; Paperless Parts: 'sales handoff through go-live and transition to Customer Success') — time-boxed, with ongoing account ownership explicitly passed to a different role (CSE) once the engagement concludes.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Concrete ~70% base/30% incentive mix tied to billable utilization; utilization is 'the dominant KPI' with 75-85% targets treated as a first-class, actively-managed number.

coding_intensity.rationale: Writing code/configurations and debugging integrations are core delivery activities, but the deliverable is a configured client solution, not shipped product code the business runs on at large.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Bimodal but real: historically 4-5 days/week on-site at consultancies, shifted post-COVID to biweekly hybrid; vendor in-house PS runs lighter.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed anywhere; delivery work is configuration/integration-focused.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware/physical resource constraints; software configuration/integration delivery.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Ladders bifurcate into business and technical tracks at senior levels, but the core IC role is evaluated on delivery/utilization metrics.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Client workshops and delivery documentation are private, engagement-specific, not public content.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Implementation work often touches cloud/CI-CD configuration for the client's environment, a real but secondary skill relative to the archetype's delivery/consulting focus.

archetypes.name: Developer Relations / Developer Advocacy
archetypes.overall_notes: Public-facing, community/ecosystem-oriented archetype with no account or ticket ownership. Sharpest contrast in the cluster on teaching_enjoyment, account_relationship_ownership, and variable_comp_appetite.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Brief names a 'DevRel metrics problem' — no single accepted success metric exists (active users, DQLs, NPS all compete); practitioners had to invent proxy metrics precisely because none were trusted.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Structured around planned content production and scheduled talks/events, though community moderation introduces some real-time responsiveness.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Not mentioned anywhere; no on-call/paging/incident-response structure described.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Some sub-variants skew toward SDK/docs/CLI improvement, but secondary to content/community work and not done under SLA pressure.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Not discussed; technical work is sample apps/POCs/SDK improvements, not platform design for scale.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Builds sample applications across whatever the product/ecosystem requires, but scoped to one company's product ecosystem.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Measured on soft/contested proxies rather than a hard owned KPI/SLO with production accountability.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Engages developers/community/internal product teams via feedback loops, but public/community-facing rather than sustained one-to-one client register.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: The archetype's defining trait: blog posts, conference talks, video content, and community moderation are the named, evaluated, primary job output.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Not an individual-relationship-based role at all; the audience is a broadcast community, not a countable portfolio of accounts.

relationship_continuity.rationale: 'DevRel is not tied to specific accounts or tickets... success is measured by community/ecosystem health' — the clearest opposite pole from CSE's named, ongoing account relationships.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp is 'overwhelmingly base + equity, with little to no sales-style variable compensation.'

coding_intensity.rationale: Writes code/sample apps, but most time goes to 'creating content, speaking at events, and building relationships.'

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Conference talks/events are a named, real activity and burnout from conference travel is documented, though some roles minimize travel via remote video.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: Not discussed anywhere; technical work is demos, SDKs, and docs.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: No mention of hardware or physical resource constraints anywhere.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Ladder tops out in Principal/Head/Director/VP with team management at the senior end, but the core IC archetype is evaluated on personal output.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: This is the archetype's true defining trait. The brief's own core framing — conference talks, published content, open community moderation as the named, evaluated, primary job output — is specifically about public, one-to-many visibility, not teaching ability in the abstract; someone who loves 1:1 customer teaching but would never want a public platform is a poor fit regardless of how well they teach.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Mixes structured cadences with genuinely undefined problems (team morale, conflict) that have no crisp metric, but is framed around known responsibilities rather than 'define the problem yourself' mandates.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: Explicitly moves 'between strategic planning and tactical firefighting within the same hour' (escalations, on-call handling as manager).

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Handles escalations from on-call, but this is secondhand exposure as manager/escalation point, not personally paging or doing live diagnosis.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: 'In the code, not writing code' with hands-on coding cadence as low as a day every 3-4 months — diagnostic depth is not a job requirement.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Reviews architectural proposals and stays connected via architectural discussion, but is evaluator, not designer.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Stays connected via code review and occasional pairing, useful for credibility but not a central evaluated competency.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Explicit team-level KPIs (velocity, MTTR, defect density, retention) owned even without personally producing the code.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Coordinates with product/design and cross-team representation, a structured-but-recurring posture, not fluid executive-to-engineer register-shifting.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: No discussion of public teaching/content; growth-plan coaching is private 1:1 mentorship.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Not discussed; EM's 'accounts' are internal reports and stakeholders, not a portfolio of client relationships.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Not discussed; EM's relationships with reports are ongoing but this isn't a client/account relationship in the sense this dimension measures.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp is 'base + bonus + equity, same shape as senior IC comp — not commission/variable-based.'

coding_intensity.rationale: Headline claim: 'in the code, not writing code,' hands-on cadence roughly a day every 3-4 months, never in the critical path.

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Not discussed; an internal, office/remote-based role with no travel component mentioned.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: No mention of threat modeling or adversarial thinking anywhere in the brief.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: Not discussed anywhere; EM's technical exposure is architectural/code-review level.

people_management_orientation.rationale: The defining trait of the entire archetype: 1:1s, growth plans, hiring, and retention/morale are the actual basis of the EM's own performance review.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: No public content or speaking expectation described; growth-plan coaching is private 1:1 mentorship.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope; EM's technical exposure is architectural/code-review level, not hands-on infra.

ambiguity_tolerance.rationale: Centers day-to-day on owning roadmap/requirements for a technical surface, making build-vs-buy and prioritization calls under ambiguous, non-numeric trade-offs — absorbed the old roadmap/prioritization-ownership concept per dimensions.json's merge notes.

interrupt_tolerance.rationale: A day full of conversations with engineers and cross-functional coordination — plannable, meeting-driven interruptions rather than an inbound queue or page.

oncall_incident_appetite.rationale: Not discussed; TPMs own API/platform reliability as a metric but there's no mention of the TPM personally being paged.

debugging_diagnostic_depth.rationale: Reads code/specs closely enough to catch bad estimates and occasionally scripts, implying light diagnostic engagement, evaluative not hands-on.

systems_design_scale.rationale: Judgment calls are 'functionally systems-design decisions' (build-vs-buy, API/data-model lifecycle), but the TPM shapes/evaluates designs rather than personally engineering them.

technical_breadth_depth.rationale: Requires fluency across APIs, SDKs, developer platforms, and ML infra, spanning multiple adjacent technical layers.

outcome_accountability.rationale: Success criteria (adoption, reliability, time-to-market) are partly attributable to engineering execution the TPM doesn't fully control.

stakeholder_client_comfort.rationale: Sustained cross-functional coordination and stakeholder satisfaction with roadmap execution as an explicit success metric, though 'customer' is mostly internal engineering teams.

teaching_enjoyment.rationale: Not discussed at all; TPM communication is internal spec-writing and design-review discussion with engineering peers.

account_portfolio_breadth.rationale: Not a discussed axis; TPMs own a product/roadmap surface, not a portfolio of client accounts.

relationship_continuity.rationale: Not a discussed axis; TPMs own a product/roadmap surface, not a persistent named client relationship.

variable_comp_appetite.rationale: Comp is 'base + bonus + equity... similar structure to senior IC engineering — not commission/variable-heavy like sales-adjacent roles.'

coding_intensity.rationale: The single most emphasized misconception: 'I'll stay technical and keep writing code' — reality is technical fluency is used 'to evaluate and shape engineering work, not to do it.'

travel_embed_willingness.rationale: Not discussed; an internal, office/remote cross-functional role with no travel component mentioned.

adversarial_threat_modeling.rationale: No mention of threat modeling or adversarial reasoning; API auth/error-handling is framed as lifecycle ownership, not adversarial thinking.

physical_constraint_engineering.rationale: Not discussed anywhere; TPM's technical surfaces are software-layer, no hardware/physical-constraint debugging mentioned.

people_management_orientation.rationale: Career ladder contrasts IC-track Principal/Staff PM against the Group PM management track; the core TPM archetype scoped here has no direct reports.

public_visibility_comfort.rationale: Not discussed at all; TPM communication is internal spec-writing and design-review discussion with engineering peers.

ml_engineering_fluency.rationale: A TPM scoped to an ML/AI platform would score higher, but this isn't the modal case across the archetype as a whole.

mobile_platform_fluency.rationale: Not relevant to this archetype's scope.

data_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: A TPM scoped to a data platform would score higher, but this isn't the modal case across the archetype as a whole.

cloud_infrastructure_fluency.rationale: A TPM scoped to an infra/developer-platform product would score higher, but this isn't the modal case across the archetype as a whole.
